title: Operations Commander — Program Management
prerequisites: Advanced Offensive Track - Mastery Tier Access
learning_objectives: Resource allocation and skill mapping across concurrent engagements
Engagement dashboard design and metrics tracking
Quality assurance processes for finding validation and reporting
Incident response playbooks for engagement issues
Knowledge management system for TTP database and lessons learned
Team development planning and skill progression
Multi-country coordination and time zone management
scenario: This Mastery mission requires professional-grade execution in Professional red team managing 5 operators across 3 simultaneous African engagements. Challenge: Develop complete operations management framework for concurrent multi-country red team engagements. Relevance: Scaling red team operations requires sophisticated resource management and quality assurance processes

subtasks.title: Incident Response Playbook
subtasks.description: Scope violations, accidental damage, operator safety.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Develop OPSEC incident procedures:
        - Detection: How do you know you've been compromised?
        - Assessment: How bad is it? What's exposed?
        - Containment: Immediate actions to limit damage
        - Decision tree: Escalation and notification procedures

        Deliver: Playbook with decision trees and response options.

resources.title: Mission Overview: Operations Commander — Program Management

reflection_prompt: How would you prioritize resources during a critical incident affecting multiple African engagements? What metrics would indicate your red team is operating at the highest level?

quiz.question: When managing concurrent red team engagements across multiple African countries with limited operators, what is the primary purpose of resource allocation planning?
options: To maximize operator workload regardless of quality
To match operator skills to engagement requirements while ensuring no single operator bottleneck exists and quality standards are maintained
Resource allocation is unnecessary if you have enough staff
All operators should work on all engagements equally
quiz.explanation: Resource allocation matches specific operator expertise to engagement requirements. Effective allocation prevents bottlenecks, ensures quality, and enables you to serve multiple clients simultaneously with diverse needs. This is critical for scaling operations across Africa.

quiz.question: In knowledge management systems for red teams, why is a TTP (Tactics, Techniques, Procedures) database considered essential for professional operations?
options: TTP databases are academic exercises with no practical value
TTPs capture operational tradecraft, enabling teams to reuse techniques across clients, avoid duplicate work, and continuously improve based on lessons learned
Individual operators should maintain separate TTP knowledge
quiz.explanation: Professional red teams maintain centralized TTP databases documenting what works, what doesn't, and lessons learned from each engagement. This institutional knowledge accelerates future operations, prevents repeating mistakes, and improves team effectiveness across concurrent engagements.

portfolio_artifacts: Evidence of technical excellence and strategic thinking
mentor_notes:
This Mastery mission requires senior mentor review. Candidates must demonstrate:
- Professional-grade execution and documentation
- Deep understanding of African organizational and threat contexts
- Strategic thinking beyond tactical execution
- Readiness for commercial red team operations
- Leadership potential within OCH and broader community

Successful completion positions candidate for Mastery portfolio and professional practice.
